summary: An outstanding audience (AUD) recording that perfectly captures a legendary 1971 show highlighted by a monster Hard to Handle and a soaring, near‑20‑minute Lovelight.
review: Fans rave about the fire‑filled performance – especially the incendiary Hard to Handle, epic The Other One > Me & My Uncle sequence, and an unforgettable Lovelight close – while Charlie Miller’s meticulous remaster delivers crystal‑clear audience ambience despite a slightly tinny drum edge and muddy low end.  The show is consistently described as tight, high‑energy and one of the best from ’71.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 enthusiastic reviews praise both the musical performance (multiple standout songs) and the recording quality (called excellent/aud‑snob favorite). Minor technical quirks are noted but do not detract from overall excellence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remastered AUD with great depth and space; drums a bit tinny, bass slightly muddy and vocals lower than ideal, but overall clear, balanced and immersive.

standout_songs: The Other One > Me & My Uncle > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, high‑energy set with a rocking second half; the band feeds off an enthusiastic crowd, delivering seamless transitions and dynamic peaks throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for soaring, clean, soulful solos (heart‑breaking guitar work) and powerful tone; some reviewers note he may have dropped to his knees during Hard to Handle.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for engaging the audience from the stage front, delivering strong vocals on Me & Bobby McGee and adding memorable banter that guides tapers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Hard‑driving drumming lauded even without Mickey; drums described as slightly tinny in the mix but overall energetic and propulsive.
